event: Early Neolithic farming villages such as Ganj Dareh and Chogha Bonut emerge on the Iranian plateau and Zagros foothills.
The proto-Elamite civilization develops urban centers and one of the world's earliest writing systems at sites like Susa.
The kingdom of Elam rises in the southwest, becoming a major power rivaling Mesopotamian states.
The Elamite king Untash-Napirisha builds the great ziggurat of Chogha Zanbil near Susa.
Iranian-speaking Medes and Persians migrate onto the plateau and establish settled tribal confederations.
The Median kingdom is consolidated under Deioces, forming the first major Iranian state.
The Medes, allied with the Babylonians, destroy Nineveh and bring down the Assyrian Empire.
Cyrus the Great overthrows the Medes and founds the Achaemenid Persian Empire.
Cyrus conquers Babylon and issues the Cyrus Cylinder, often cited as an early charter of tolerance.
Darius I seizes the throne and expands the Achaemenid Empire to its greatest extent, from the Indus to the Aegean.
Persian forces under Darius I are defeated by the Greeks at the Battle of Marathon.
Xerxes I invades Greece, winning at Thermopylae but suffering naval defeat at Salamis.
Alexander the Great defeats Darius III and burns Persepolis, ending the Achaemenid Empire.
The Seleucid Empire founded by Alexander's general Seleucus takes control of Persia.
The Parthian (Arsacid) Empire is founded by Arsaces I, gradually freeing Iran from Seleucid rule.
The Parthians annihilate a Roman army under Crassus at the Battle of Carrhae.
Ardashir I overthrows the Parthians and founds the Sasanian Empire, reviving Zoroastrian Persian statehood.
Shapur I defeats and captures the Roman emperor Valerian at the Battle of Edessa.
Khosrow I Anushirvan begins a golden age of Sasanian reform, justice, and learning.
Arab Muslim armies decisively defeat the Sasanians at Nahavand, opening Iran to Islamic rule.
The death of the last Sasanian king Yazdegerd III completes the Arab conquest of Iran.
The Samanid dynasty rises in the east, fostering a revival of Persian language and culture.
Ferdowsi completes the Shahnameh, the Persian national epic that preserves Iranian myth and history.
The Seljuk Turks establish an empire over Iran, supporting Persian administration and Sunni Islam.
Genghis Khan's Mongol invasion devastates Iranian cities, killing millions and destroying irrigation systems.
Hulagu Khan founds the Ilkhanate, establishing Mongol rule over Iran.
Timur (Tamerlane) begins his conquest of Iran, founding the Timurid dynasty.
Shah Ismail I founds the Safavid dynasty and makes Twelver Shia Islam the state religion.
Shah Abbas I moves the capital to Isfahan and builds it into a magnificent imperial city.
Afghan invaders capture Isfahan, triggering the collapse of the Safavid Empire.
Nader Shah seizes the throne, founds the Afsharid dynasty, and later sacks Delhi.
Agha Mohammad Khan founds the Qajar dynasty and makes Tehran the capital.
The Treaty of Turkmenchay cedes Caucasus territories to Russia after defeat in the Russo-Persian wars.
The Constitutional Revolution forces the shah to grant a constitution and establish a parliament (Majles).
Oil is discovered at Masjed Soleyman, the first major find in the Middle East.
Reza Khan overthrows the Qajars and founds the Pahlavi dynasty, launching rapid modernization.
Reza Shah officially requests that the country be called Iran internationally instead of Persia.
Britain and the Soviet Union invade Iran, forcing Reza Shah to abdicate in favor of his son Mohammad Reza.
Iran becomes a founding member of the United Nations.
Prime Minister Mohammad Mossadegh nationalizes the Anglo-Iranian Oil Company.
A CIA- and MI6-backed coup overthrows Mossadegh and restores the shah's power.
The shah launches the White Revolution of land reform and social modernization.
The Islamic Revolution topples the shah and Ayatollah Khomeini establishes an Islamic Republic.
Iraq invades Iran, beginning a devastating eight-year war.
The Iran-Iraq War ends in a ceasefire after roughly a million deaths and no territorial change.
Ayatollah Khomeini dies and Ali Khamenei becomes Supreme Leader.
Reformist Mohammad Khatami is elected president, raising hopes for political liberalization.
Iran's clandestine nuclear enrichment program at Natanz is publicly revealed, triggering international concern.
Disputed re-election of President Ahmadinejad sparks mass Green Movement protests.
Iran signs the JCPOA nuclear agreement with world powers, easing sanctions in exchange for limits on enrichment.
A U.S. drone strike in Baghdad kills Revolutionary Guard commander Qasem Soleimani.
The death of Mahsa Amini in custody ignites nationwide 'Woman, Life, Freedom' protests.
